title: Immunization, HepB3 (% of one-year-old children)
definition: Child immunization rate, hepatitis B is the percentage of children ages 12-23 months who received hepatitis B vaccinations before 12 months or at any time before the survey. A child is considered adequately immunized after three doses.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization:
World Health Organization (WHO), uri: http://www.who.int/immunization/monitoring_surveillance/en/;
UN Children's Fund (UNICEF)

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for hepatitis b immunization.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Turkiye, Turkmenistan, Portugal, while the lightest include Sudan, Central African Republic, Papua New Guinea; that contrast is the fastest way to read the hepatitis b immunization pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Hepatitis B immunization may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: DPT immunization can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Measles immunization can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Rural open defecation overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: too close to DPT and measles immunization to justify another vaccine map in beta.
